name: Contratto (Cantine Contratto)
visitPolicy: advance booking online; structured tour with tasting
blurb: Founded in 1867, Contratto is one of the oldest Italian sparkling wine houses, and its underground cellars, dug by hand into the tufa hillside beneath Canelli over a ten-year period starting in 1899, were recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2014 as part of the Paesaggi Vitivinicoli del Piemonte designation. The cellars are cavernous, more than 30 meters deep, and the comparison to a wine cathedral is not press-copy exaggeration. Alta Langa Metodo Classico is the undersung answer to Franciacorta, and Contratto makes a strong case for why.
